잠언 27:1.content: Don’t boast about tomorrow; for you don’t know what a day may bring.

잠언 27:2.content: Let another man praise you, and not your own mouth; a stranger, and not your own lips.

잠언 27:3.content: A stone is heavy, and sand is a burden; but a fool’s provocation is heavier than both.

잠언 27:4.content: Wrath is cruel, and anger is overwhelming; but who is able to stand before jealousy?

잠언 27:5.content: Better is open rebuke than hidden love.

잠언 27:6.content: The wounds of a friend are faithful, although the kisses of an enemy are profuse.

잠언 27:7.content: A full soul loathes a honeycomb; but to a hungry soul, every bitter thing is sweet.

잠언 27:8.content: As a bird that wanders from her nest, so is a man who wanders from his home.

잠언 27:9.content: Perfume and incense bring joy to the heart; so does earnest counsel from a man’s friend.

잠언 27:10.content: Don’t forsake your friend and your father’s friend. Don’t go to your brother’s house in the day of your disaster. A neighbor who is near is better than a distant brother.

잠언 27:11.content: Be wise, my son, and bring joy to my heart, then I can answer my tormentor.

잠언 27:12.content: A prudent man sees danger and takes refuge; but the simple pass on, and suffer for it.

잠언 27:13.content: Take his garment when he puts up collateral for a stranger. Hold it for a wayward woman!

잠언 27:14.content: He who blesses his neighbor with a loud voice early in the morning, it will be taken as a curse by him.

잠언 27:15.content: A continual dropping on a rainy day and a contentious wife are alike:

잠언 27:16.content: restraining her is like restraining the wind, or like grasping oil in his right hand.

잠언 27:17.content: Iron sharpens iron; so a man sharpens his friend’s countenance.

잠언 27:18.content: Whoever tends the fig tree shall eat its fruit. He who looks after his master shall be honored.

잠언 27:19.content: Like water reflects a face, so a man’s heart reflects the man.

잠언 27:20.content: Sheol and Abaddon are never satisfied; and a man’s eyes are never satisfied.

잠언 27:21.content: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace for gold; but man is refined by his praise.

잠언 27:22.content: Though you grind a fool in a mortar with a pestle along with grain, yet his foolishness will not be removed from him.

잠언 27:23.content: Know well the state of your flocks, and pay attention to your herds:

잠언 27:24.content: for riches are not forever, nor does the crown endure to all generations.

잠언 27:25.content: The hay is removed, and the new growth appears, the grasses of the hills are gathered in.

잠언 27:26.content: The lambs are for your clothing, and the goats are the price of a field.

잠언 27:27.content: There will be plenty of goats’ milk for your food, for your family’s food, and for the nourishment of your servant girls.
